canonical_definition: Across the provided literature, 'Temporal consistency', often used interchangeably with 'Temporal coherence', is predominantly discussed in the context of video generation and analysis. The most common framing defines it as the quality of a video maintaining a smooth and logically continuous progression of events and objects across frames, without flicker or discontinuities. A more specific application within this domain is in video segmentation, where the construct refers to producing object segmentation masks that are stable and coherent across consecutive frames. A distinct usage appears in the context of text, where one paper defines 'Temporal coherence' as the 'logical and chronological flow of ideas across sentences'. The construct is operationalized and assessed through various metrics depending on the modality. For video, papers mention the use of CHScore to evaluate 'logical progression and continuity' and Flow-score (FScore). In the domain of text, the BBScoreV2 is noted for its ability to 'capture temporal structure' and 'assess document coherence'.
papers: Active Layer-Contrastive Decoding Reduces Hallucination in Large Language Model Generation
Mapping the Minds ofLLMs: A Graph-Based Analysis of ReasoningLLMs
K-HALU: Multiple Answer Korean Hallucination Benchmark for Large Language Models
SageAttention: Accurate 8-Bit Attention for Plug-and-play Inference Acceleration
ChronoMagic-Bench: A Benchmark for Metamorphic Evaluation of Text-to-Time-lapse Video Generation
GenRL: Multimodal-foundation world models for generalization in embodied agents
One Token to Seg Them All: Language Instructed Reasoning Segmentation in Videos

definitions.text: The extent to which a generated video maintains smooth, logically continuous progression across frames without flicker or discontinuities.
definitions.paper: ChronoMagic-Bench: A Benchmark for Metamorphic Evaluation of Text-to-Time-lapse Video Generation

definitions.text: The quality of producing segmentation masks for an object that are stable and coherent across consecutive video frames.
definitions.paper: One Token to Seg Them All: Language Instructed Reasoning Segmentation in Videos

relationships.paper: One Token to Seg Them All: Language Instructed Reasoning Segmentation in Videos
